title: eriogonum sphaerocephalum subsp. tenue
intro: Eriogonum sphaerocephalum subsp. tenue, also known as Tenleaf Sulphur-flower Buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum sphaerocephalum subsp. tenue include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
